details.text: Derivative: عَبَسَ (He frowned) – In the Arabic language there are different words for frowning. عَبَسَ refers to the perturbed expression that only occurs between the eyes and the lower part of the forehead whilst not including a twisting of the face or mouth. As such, عَبَسَ - عُبُوس reflects the minimal level of expressing frustration. The use of the third person عَبَسَ “He frowned” rather than the first person عَبَسْتَ “You frowned” reflects a style of lightening the impact or severity of the criticism.

Derivative: تَلَهَّىٰ comes from the word لَهْو (Lahw) which can be understood in English to refer to entertainment or a distraction. In origin it refers to something that keeps you away from a more important matter or task. تَلَهَّىٰ means to be engaged in an activity which is not as important i.e., to be distracted from what is more important or worthy.

Derivative: كَلَّا can be indicative of scolding and repudiation (زَجْر - Zajr) for an act that was undertaken, with the meaning being something like “No! The matter is not like that” or “No! The approach should not be like that”. كَلَّا can also carry the meaning in classical Arabic of حَقَّا (Haqqan) which means: “Truly” in the sense of “there is no doubt about it”.

Derivative: تَذْكِرَةٌ is an accentuated form of تَذْكِير (to remind or reminder) and is the most powerful form from the words related to being a reminder which stem from the root of ذَكَرَ. تَذْكِرَةٌ therefore refers to an incredibly powerful reminder with there being no reminder that is more powerful than it. This تَذْكِرَةٌ refers to Al-Qur’an.
Derivative: صُحُفٍ literally means scriptures. It comes from صَحِيفَة which is something that is spread out and is written upon like a parchment or scroll.
Derivative: مُكَرَّمَةٍ means honoured and noble and comes from تَكْرِيم in the Arabic language.

Derivative: سَفَرَةٍ means scribes which comes from سِفْر which means a book that unveils things. It is in reference to the Angels. It is also said that سَفَرَةٍ is the plural of سَافِر with the meaning of سَفِير which means an emissary or messenger.

Derivative: بَرَرَةٍ is the plural of بَرٌّ (Barr) and also the plural of بَارٌّ (Baarr). بَرٌّ (Barr) means goodness and someone who is extremely good and is the accentuate hyperbolised form. بَارٌّ refers to someone who is good but not as powerful as بَرٌّ. أَبْرَار (Abraar) is the usual plural for بَارٌّ (Baarr) but بَرَرَةٍ (Bararah) is a more powerful plural form for it. بَرَرَةٍ refers to those who are most righteous and is in reference to the Angels.

Derivative: قُتِلَ is in the past (الماضِي) passive (مَبْنِي عَلى المَجْهُول) tense. It literally means “was killed” and is used to mean “May he be killed” as a curse against him, like “May he be cursed”.

- This is called أُسلُوب التَّعَجُّب (Usloob At-Ta’ajjub) which is a form to express shock, amazement or anger at something. مَا أَكْفَرَ means “How incredible his disbelief is!” or “How great his disbelief is!”. Another view is that the مَا is for the purpose of questioning (الاِسْتِفْهام - Al-Istifhaam) in which case مَا أَكْفَرَ would mean: “What has made him or led him to disbelieve?”.
Derivative: نُطْفَةٍ refers to a sperm-drop and it implies something that is miniscule and insignificant in contrast to what results from it.

Derivative: قَدَّرَ – تَقْدِيرًا: Means to make an evaluation or determination based on very precise calculations. قَدَّرَ here refers to the process that Allah takes the Nutfah (insignificant fluid) and transforms it into the form of a complete human being based on precise determinants.
Derivative: يَسَّرَ means to make easy and facilitate.
Derivative: السَّبِيلَ means “the path” and here it can refer to the path from his mother’s belly, or the path into the life of this world, or the path to guidance.
Derivative: أَقْبَرَ means to bury or make a grave for. قَبَرَ means to bury into the earth and every death regardless of where and how it took place ultimately ends up in the earth.
Derivative: أَنْشَرَ means to raise up and resurrect. It is in the past form of the verb which is used in Arabic to refer to the future to imply the certainty of the occurrence action or event.

Derivative: لَمَّا negates the verb that follows but with the meaning of “not yet” or “still not”. There is an implication in لَمَّا that there is still hope and that the negation can be lifted i.e., in this verse it means: “The human has not yet fulfilled what Allah commanded him with”.
Derivative: يَقْضِ from قَضَى – يَقْضِي – قَضاء which means to undertake a task to its completion.
Derivative: يَنظُرِ means to look at but also means to examine something by looking at deeply. Here يَنظُرِ means to think deeply about.
Derivative: صَبَبْنَا from صَبَّ – صَبًّا means to pour out water in abundance. Here it means to cause the rain to fall down in abundance.

Derivative: شَقَقْنَا comes from شّقَّ – شَقًّا. شّقَّ (Shaqqa) means to split, cleave or rip open and is used in relation to things that you don’t usually associate with be split or ripped open i.e., like the earth, rocks and sky.
Derivative: أَنْبَتْنَا “We caused to grow” is from أَنْبَتَ – إِنْبات which means to ensure to grow meticulously. It is also used in relation to a child that matures quickly.
Derivative: قَضْبًا refers to all kinds of vegetation and mostly those which grow under the soil.

Derivative: يَزَّكَّىٰ is the shortened form of يَتَزَكَّى and means to purify and cleanse oneself, which can imply bettering oneself. The shortened form can also carry a subtle implication of being less in impact than the full form. For example, يَتَزَكَّى can imply being fully purified or more completely purified, whilst يَزَّكَّىٰ can imply gaining some level of purification or partial purification.
Derivative: لَعَلَّ – implies the meaning of “The hope that perhaps (i.e., that something will happen)”.

- “What will give you any clue or idea” and is commonly translated to mean: “What will make you know” or “What will inform you”. دَرَى – يَدْرِي means “to know”. مَا يُدْرِيكَ implies the meaning: “What do you have at your disposal or what means do you have to allow you to attain that information or knowledge?” and as such implies that he has no such tools or means available to allow him to know or attain the information.
Derivative: حَدَائِقَ is the plural of حَدِيقَة and refers to gardens which are usually fenced off.

Derivative: غُلْبًا is the plural of أَغْلَب and it means thick and dense. In relation to gardens it refers to large trees that are grouped or packed densely together and their branches are intertwined i.e., extremely lush gardens.

Derivative: فَاكِهَةً refers to delicious fruit in classical Arabic and originates from the verb فَكِهَ which means to be overjoyed. فَاكِهَةً is a fruit that brings joy to the one who eats it. It has been stated that the term فَاكِهَةً excludes dates, pomegranates and dates.
Derivative: أَبًّا refers to grass which animals eat from.
Derivative: الصَّاخَّةُ means a deafening loud scream.
Derivative: يَفِرُّ from فَرَّ – فِرارًا which means to flee or run away and is used when someone is running away from something due to fear or being terrified by something.

Derivative: يُغْنِي means to suffice and be enough. Here it implies that he will be so consumed by his own affair and situation that he will not have a concern for anything else i.e., his affair is enough for him.
Derivative: امْرِئٍ is the same as المَرْءٌ and it refers to a man. It comes from مُرُوءَة (Muroo’ah) which refers to chivalry and noble virtues associated with a man.
Derivative: مُسْفِرَةٌ means to be lit up and beaming brightly. سَفَرَ means to unveil or uncover. مُسْفِرَةٌ which means to shine and be lit up can be seen to be the result of the veil being lifted.
Derivative: مُسْتَبْشِرَةٌ from اِسْتِبْشار which means to rejoice and be full of delight because of good news that has reached you.
Derivative: ضَاحِكَةٌ means laughing in a full manner i.e., with accompanied by noise and showing of teeth.

Derivative: الذِّكْرَىٰ “The reminder or remembrance”. ذِكْرَى is the مُبَالَغَة (More powerful and emphasised) form of ذِكْر. As such ذِكْرَى (Dhikraa) can refer to powerful remembrance or reminder whilst ذِكْر (Dhikr) refers to remembrance or reminder.

Derivative: يَذَّكَّرُ is the shortened form of يَتَذَكَرُ which means to remember, be reminded, receive a reminder or make effort to be reminded. In its short form it implies “some level of” reminder for himself i.e., something that would make him think.
Derivative: غَبَرَةٌ means dust and can also refer to a darkness and grimness from the intensity of anguish, distress and grief that they are overcome with.
Derivative: قَتَرَةٌ is used to refer to black smoke that emits from fire when burning wood or cooking something.
Derivative: تَرْهَقُ – رَهْق (Rahq) in Arabic refers to something forcefully overshadowing or climbing over something else. تَرْهَقُ means to climb over and cover.
Derivative: الْفَجَرَةُ refers to those who wilfully disobey Allah in the most overt and rebellious manner in their acts and speech.

Derivative: اسْتَغْنَىٰ is the one who has اِسْتِغْناء which means to view oneself to be self-sufficient and free of need. The person who is غَنِي is the one who is not worried about the costs of life and has no world concerns. One of Allah’s names is الغَنِي because He is free of need. اسْتَغْنَىٰ here implies that the person has no sincere interest or care (i.e., to listen, receive guidance, purify himself etc.) and considers himself to be free from benefiting from someone or something else.

Derivative: تَصَدَّىٰ – صَدى refers to an echo or reverberation i.e., something that comes back to you. تَصَدَّىٰ means to be relentless upon something and to go back to something over and over again just like the echo keeps coming back to your ear. It can therefore imply the meaning of: “Going out of your way”.
- مَا عَلَيْكَ means that there will be no consequences against you and you will not be held responsible for.
Derivative: يَسْعَىٰ means to move at a pace that is faster than walking and slower than running. In this context it implies eagerness, yearning and enthusiasm i.e., to attain goodness and guidance.
